mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0 percent indicates severe labor market slack and depressed consumer spending, which suppresses corporate revenues and drives negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent over a 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which translates into higher earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which signals near-term earnings momentum and generates positive equity returns within a one-week horizon during growth phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when applied outside late-cycle expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 217.0 signal entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and driving negative returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 217.0 over a 60-day forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, generating positive equity returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of high-payroll periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.0 percent confirms sustained economic expansion, which boosts aggregate corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over the next month during stable growth regimes.
failure_condition: Predictive accuracy drops below 50 percent or Rank IC < 0.02 when applied outside stable growth regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and rising unemployment signals structural labor market inefficiencies and impending economic contraction, which suppresses equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint divergence between INDPRO and UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.
